summary.what: Address code-review findings on skip-rule changes
summary.why: Reviewer flagged collision risk in short-SHA revert matching, double-scan inefficiency in pending detection, and a divergence between the visibility count and the actual filter — all real correctness/consistency bugs that needed fixing before merge.
summary.how: Bumped revert SHA minimum to 12 chars in regex+matcher (collision-safe), threaded a single ListEntries call through GetPendingCommits via documentedSHASetFromEntries (pure helper) so both latest-entry resolution and revert auto-skipping share one disk scan, and unified the count helper with filterCommits so visibility tracks reality.

notes: Reviewer also called out that NewStorage now does I/O at construction (.timbersignore load) — chose to document this in the godoc rather than refactor to lazy load, because the I/O is one os.Open of a tiny file and a lazy path would hide state behind first-use side effects. The trivial 'label-only' verbose status test is now backed by a real integration case (writes an entry, makes a .gitignore-only commit, asserts JSON count == 1). Extracted realGitOps to gitops.go to keep storage.go under the 350-line lint budget after the new helpers landed.
